Data analysis of the student AI for DS Survey data

library(dplyr)
library(janitor)
library(ggplot2)
library(rlang)
library(ggmosaic)
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr) 
library(forcats)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
 setwd("C:/Users/amhasan1/OneDrive - North Carolina A&T State University/Desktop/AI4DS_Survey_Paper/Student_Survey_October_2025")

library(readxl)


# Read the data normally, letting readxl decide column count
Student <- read_excel("C:/Users/amhasan1/OneDrive - North Carolina A&T State University/Desktop/AI4DS_Survey_Paper/Student_Survey_October_2025/AI4DS-Student-Survey_October 5, 2025_18.02.xlsx")

# Extract short and long names
short <- read_excel("AI4DS-Student-Survey_October 5, 2025_18.02.xlsx", 
                    range = "A1:BN1", col_names = FALSE) |> unlist()

long <- read_excel("AI4DS-Student-Survey_October 5, 2025_18.02.xlsx", 
                   range = "A2:BN2", col_names = FALSE) |> unlist()

# Make sure both match the actual column count
n_cols <- ncol(Student)
short <- short[1:n_cols]
long  <- long[1:n_cols]

# Reload the data, skipping first 2 rows, and apply short names
StudentDF <- read_excel("AI4DS-Student-Survey_October 5, 2025_18.02.xlsx", skip = 2, col_names = short)

# Map short names to long labels
header_labels <- setNames(long, short)

# Filter data
FilteredDF <- StudentDF %>%
  filter(`Duration (in seconds)` >= 120, Progress >= 25)

# Print dimensions + sample size
cat("Remaining sample size:", nrow(FilteredDF), "students\n")
## Remaining sample size: 120 students
dim(FilteredDF)
## [1] 120  66

set up for the colors

library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(forcats); library(ggplot2); library(scales)
## 
## Attaching package: 'scales'
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     discard
## The following object is masked from 'package:readr':
## 
##     col_factor
## use this palette for all next figures
palette_redteal <- c("#117A65","#48C9B0","#F1C40F","#E67E22","#8B0000" )

#       plot.margin     = margin(10, 15, 15, 15)
#     )
# }

Saving custom theme for future use

# --- Define once at the top of your script or in a separate R file ---
theme_pub <- function(base_size = 10) {
  theme_classic(base_size = base_size) +
    theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray25"),
      axis.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      axis.text.y     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10, lineheight = 1.1),
      axis.title.x    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      axis.title.y    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray90"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.text     = element_text(size = 8),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.key.size = unit(0.35, "cm"),
      legend.margin   = margin(t = 2, b = 5),
      legend.box.margin = margin(5, 5, 10, 5),
      legend.box = "vertical",
      plot.caption    = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0, color = "gray30"),
      plot.margin     = margin(10, 15, 45, 15)
    )
}

###
load_pub_style <- function() {
  # Color palette
  assign("pal_q", c(
    "Not at All Familiar" = "#B2182B",
    "Not Very Familiar"   = "#E67E22",
    "Neutral"             = "#F1C40F",
    "Somewhat Familiar"   = "#48C9B0",
    "Very Familiar"       = "#117A65"
  ), envir = .GlobalEnv)
  
  # Theme
  theme_set(theme_pub())
}

load_pub_style()

Demographics sex table Q3-6

## Demographics table Q3-6
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# ---- 1) Q3 Summary: Student Sex ----
q3_summary <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q3)) %>%
  tabyl(Q3) %>%
  adorn_totals("row") %>%
  adorn_percentages("col") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 3)


# ---- 1) Q3 Summary: Student Sex ----


# total valid responses
n_valid_q3 <- FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q3)) %>% nrow()

q3_summary <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q3)) %>%
  tabyl(Q3) %>%                 # gives Q3 + n
  adorn_totals("row") %>%       # adds Total row
  mutate(
    percent = n / n_valid_q3,   # keep counts in `n`, compute percent separately
    percent = percent(percent, accuracy = 0.1)  # format, e.g., "61.2%"
  )

q3_summary
##                         Q3   n percent
##                     Female  71   61.2%
##                       Male  40   34.5%
##  Non-binary / third gender   2    1.7%
##          Prefer not to say   2    1.7%
##                Trans woman   1    0.9%
##                      Total 116  100.0%
# Display as a formatted table
kable(q3_summary, caption = "Q3: Student Sex - Counts and Percentages") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "center", font_size = 13)
Q3: Student Sex - Counts and Percentages
Q3 n percent
Female 71 61.2%
Male 40 34.5%
Non-binary / third gender 2 1.7%
Prefer not to say 2 1.7%
Trans woman 1 0.9%
Total 116 100.0% 
##

# Optional bar plot
ggplot(FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q3)), aes(x = Q3)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +
   coord_flip()+
  geom_text(stat = "count", aes(label = ..count..), vjust = 1, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title = "Student Sex",
    x = NULL, y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 25, 15)
  )

## major

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(janitor)
library(ggplot2)
library(kableExtra)
library(forcats)


# ---- 2) Q4 Summary (e.g., Major, Class Level, or Other Demographic) ----
q4_summary <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q4)) %>%
  tabyl(Q4) %>%
  adorn_totals("row") %>%
  adorn_percentages("col") %>%
  adorn_pct_formatting(digits = 3)

# ---- 1) Clean and consolidate major names ----
cleaned_majors <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q4)) %>%
  mutate(
    major_clean = str_to_lower(Q4),  # make lowercase
    major_clean = str_squish(major_clean),  # remove double spaces
    
    # Consolidate common variations
    major_group = case_when(
      str_detect(major_clean, "stat") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "data") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "analytic") ~ "Statistics & Data Science",
      
      str_detect(major_clean, "computer") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "cs") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "software") ~ "Computer Science / IT",
      
      str_detect(major_clean, "engineer") ~ "Engineering (All Types)",
      
      str_detect(major_clean, "math") ~ "Mathematics",
      
      str_detect(major_clean, "bio") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "life") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "chem") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "enviro") ~ "Biological / Physical Sciences",
      
      str_detect(major_clean, "business") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "finance") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "account") |
      str_detect(major_clean, "econ") ~ "Business / Economics",
      
      str_detect(major_clean, "psych") ~ "Psychology",
      str_detect(major_clean, "edu") ~ "Education",
      str_detect(major_clean, "nurs") | str_detect(major_clean, "health") ~ "Health Sciences",
      
      TRUE ~ "Other / Undeclared"
    )
  )

# ---- 2) Summarize counts and percentages ----
major_summary <- cleaned_majors %>%
  count(major_group, name = "Count") %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Count / sum(Count),
    Percent_Label = paste0(round(Percent * 100, 1), "%")
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Count))

# ---- 3) Clean, publication-ready table ----
kable(
  major_summary,
  caption = "Q4: Student Majors (Consolidated Categories)",
  col.names = c("Major Category", "Count", "Percent", "Percent Label"),
  digits = 1,
  align = "lccr"
) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "center", font_size = 13)
Q4: Student Majors (Consolidated Categories)
Major Category Count Percent Percent Label
Other / Undeclared 32 0.3 29.6%
Computer Science / IT 26 0.2 24.1%
Statistics & Data Science 26 0.2 24.1%
Biological / Physical Sciences 11 0.1 10.2%
Engineering (All Types) 7 0.1 6.5%
Health Sciences 3 0.0 2.8%
Mathematics 2 0.0 1.9%
Psychology 1 0.0 0.9% 
# ---- 4) Optional: Horizontal bar chart ----
ggplot(major_summary, aes(y = fct_reorder(major_group, Count), x = Count)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")),
            hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Q4: Student Majors (Consolidated Categories)",
    x = "Number of Students",
    y = "Major Category"
  ) +
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 35, 15)
  )

# ---- 5) (Optional) Save the figure ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q4_student_majors_consolidated.png",
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

Demographics table Q3-6

## Demographics table Q3-6
# 1) Replace NA in Q5 with "Graduate" and add it as a level
FilteredDF <- FilteredDF %>%
  mutate(
   # Q5 = replace_na(Q5, "Graduate Student"),
    Q5 = factor(Q5, levels = c("Freshman", "Sophomore", "Junior", "Senior", "Graduate Student")))



# 4) Q5 summary now includes former NAs as "Graduate"
FilteredDF %>% tabyl(Q5)
##                Q5  n    percent valid_percent
##          Freshman  4 0.03333333    0.03846154
##         Sophomore 24 0.20000000    0.23076923
##            Junior 21 0.17500000    0.20192308
##            Senior 36 0.30000000    0.34615385
##  Graduate Student 19 0.15833333    0.18269231
##              <NA> 16 0.13333333            NA
# 5) Q5 bar chart (no NA filter needed; includes "Graduate")
FilteredDF %>%
  count(Q5) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Q5, y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  ggtitle("What is your classification?") +
  labs(x = "Classification", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

Q6

## Q6 
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
library(janitor)

# ---- 1) Define ordered factor levels ----
FilteredDF$Q6 <- factor(
  FilteredDF$Q6,
  levels = c("Never", "Rarely", "Sometimes", "Often", "Very Often")
)

# ---- 2) Compute frequencies and percentages ----
plot_df_q6 <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q6)) %>%
  count(Q6) %>%
  mutate(Percent = n / sum(n))

# ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart with publication theme ----
p_q6 <- ggplot(plot_df_q6, aes(y = fct_reorder(Q6, Percent), x = Percent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline percentage labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(Percent * 100, 1), "%")),
    hjust = -0.15, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(plot_df_q6$Percent) + 0.08),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and labels
  labs(
    title = "Q6: Frequency of Using AI Technologies in Data Science Work",
    subtitle = "Includes tools such as ChatGPT, Copilot, and Google Colab",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents",
    y = "Frequency",
    caption = "Note: Percentages calculated from valid (non-missing) responses."
  ) +

  # Apply unified publication theme
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 4) Save as high-resolution PNG ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q6_ai_usage_frequency_horizontal.png",
  plot = p_q6,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q6)

Q7 Percents based on valid responses

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(forcats)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(stringr)

# ---- 1) Split and clean comma-separated responses ----
long_Q7 <- FilteredDF %>%
  select(Q7) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q7)) %>%
  mutate(Q7 = str_split(Q7, ",")) %>%
  unnest(Q7) %>%
  mutate(Q7 = str_trim(Q7))

# ---- 2) Count responses and compute percent based on valid respondents ----
n_valid <- nrow(FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q7)))

Q7_summary <- long_Q7 %>%
  count(Q7, sort = TRUE) %>%
  rename(Response = Q7, Count = n) %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Count / n_valid,
    Label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")
  )

# ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart with counts + percentages ----
p_q7 <- ggplot(Q7_summary, aes(
  y = fct_reorder(Response, Percent),
  x = Percent
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline labels showing count and percent
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Label),
    hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(Q7_summary$Percent) + 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and labels
  labs(
    title = "Q7: Common Use Cases for AI Tools in DS Tasks",
    subtitle = "Multiple selections allowed; percentages based on valid respondents",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents Selecting Each Use Case",
    y = "Use Case",
    caption = paste0("n = ", n_valid, 
                     " respondents (percentages may exceed 100% because multiple choices were allowed).")
  ) +

  # Minimal publication-style theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 25, 25, 15)
  )

# Display chart
p_q7

## Generic function for univariate Barplot plots

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(forcats)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(stringr)

# ---- Function Template with Auto-Save ----
plot_multi_response <- function(data, col, title, xlab, ylab, save_dir = "Studnt_figs_10") {
  
  # Capture column name as symbol and string
  col_sym <- rlang::ensym(col)
  col_name <- rlang::as_name(col_sym)
  
  # ---- 1) Split and clean comma-separated responses ----
  long_df <- data %>%
    select({{ col_sym }}) %>%
    filter(!is.na(!!col_sym)) %>%
    mutate(value = str_split(!!col_sym, ",")) %>%
    unnest(value) %>%
    mutate(value = str_trim(value))
  
  # ---- 2) Count responses and compute percent based on valid respondents ----
  n_valid <- data %>% filter(!is.na(!!col_sym)) %>% nrow()
  
  summary_df <- long_df %>%
    count(value, sort = TRUE) %>%
    rename(Response = value, Count = n) %>%
    mutate(
      Percent = Count / n_valid,
      Label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")
    )
  
  # ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart with counts + percentages ----
  p <- ggplot(summary_df, aes(
    y = fct_reorder(Response, Percent),
    x = Percent
  )) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +
    geom_text(aes(label = Label),
              hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black") +
    scale_x_continuous(
      labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
      limits = c(0, max(summary_df$Percent) + 0.1),
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = "Multiple selections allowed; percentages based on valid respondents",
      x = xlab,
      y = ylab,
      caption = paste0("n = ", n_valid,
                       " respondents (percentages may exceed 100% because multiple choices were allowed).")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 25, 25, 15)
    )
  
  # ---- 4) Auto-Save Plot ----
  # Create directory if it doesn't exist
  if (!dir.exists(save_dir)) dir.create(save_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  
  file_name <- paste0(save_dir, "/", col_name, ".png")
  
  ggsave(
    filename = file_name,
    plot = p,
    dpi = 600,
    width = 8.5,
    height = 5.5,
    units = "in"
  )
  
  message("✅ Plot saved as: ", file_name)
  
  # ---- 5) Return plot and data ----
  return(list(plot = p, summary = summary_df, file = file_name))
}

appliy generic function to Q6

# Example: Apply to Q6
result_q6 <- plot_multi_response(
  data = FilteredDF,
  col = Q6,
  title = "Q6: Frequency of Using AI Technologies in Data Science Work",
  xlab = "Percentage of Respondents",
  ylab = "Frequency"
)
## ✅ Plot saved as: Studnt_figs_10/Q6.png
# Display the plot
result_q6$plot

# View the summary table
result_q6$summary
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   Response   Count Percent Label     
##   <chr>      <int>   <dbl> <chr>     
## 1 Sometimes     42   0.362 42 (36.2%)
## 2 Often         24   0.207 24 (20.7%)
## 3 Never         18   0.155 18 (15.5%)
## 4 Very Often    18   0.155 18 (15.5%)
## 5 Rarely        14   0.121 14 (12.1%)

appliy generic function to Q7

# Apply to Q7
result_q7 <- plot_multi_response(
  data = FilteredDF,
  col = Q7,
  title = "Q7: Common Use Cases for AI Tools in DS Tasks",
  xlab = "Percentage of Respondents Selecting Each Use Case",
  ylab = "Use Case"
)
## ✅ Plot saved as: Studnt_figs_10/Q7.png
# Display the plot
result_q7$plot

# View the summary table
result_q7$summary
## # A tibble: 7 × 4
##   Response              Count Percent Label     
##   <chr>                 <int>   <dbl> <chr>     
## 1 Help with coding         77   0.694 77 (69.4%)
## 2 Proof reading            58   0.523 58 (52.3%)
## 3 Mathematical Proofs      41   0.369 41 (36.9%)
## 4 Literature Review        34   0.306 34 (30.6%)
## 5 Getting initial draft    31   0.279 31 (27.9%)
## 6 Other                    14   0.126 14 (12.6%)
## 7 please specify           14   0.126 14 (12.6%)

Q8: Are you interested in pursuing a degree in data science in the future?

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
library(janitor)

# ---- 1) Define ordered factor levels ----
FilteredDF$Q8 <- factor(
  FilteredDF$Q8,
  levels = c("Yes", "No", "Not sure")
)

# ---- 2) Compute frequencies and percentages ----
plot_df_q8 <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q8)) %>%
  count(Q8) %>%
  mutate(Percent = n / sum(n))

# ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart (publication theme) ----
p_q8 <- ggplot(plot_df_q8, aes(y = fct_reorder(Q8, Percent), x = Percent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline percentage labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(Percent * 100, 1), "%")),
    hjust = -0.15, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(plot_df_q8$Percent) + 0.08),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and labels
  labs(
    title = "Q8: Interest in Pursuing a Degree in Data Science",
    subtitle = "Responses show future interest in formal data science education",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents",
    y = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages calculated from valid (non-missing) responses."
  ) +

  # Unified publication theme
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 4) Save as high-resolution PNG ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q8_interest_in_ds_degree_horizontal.png",
  plot = p_q8,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q8)

Q9: Are you interested in getting a job in data science in the future?

FilteredDF$Q9 <- factor(FilteredDF$Q9, 
                        levels = c("Yes", "No", "Not sure"))

FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q9)) %>% tabyl(Q9)
##        Q9  n   percent
##       Yes 52 0.4684685
##        No 31 0.2792793
##  Not sure 28 0.2522523
FilteredDF %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Q9)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(Q9))+
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue")+
  ggtitle("Q9: Are you interested in getting a job in data science in \nthe future?")

## Barchart with percentages
FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q9)) %>%
  count(Q9) %>%
  mutate(Percent = n / sum(n) * 100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Q9, y = Percent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  ggtitle("Q9: Are you interested in getting a job in data science in \nthe future?") +
  ylab("Percentage") +
  xlab("Response") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(scale = 1))  # Format y-axis as percentage

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
library(janitor)

# ---- 1) Define response order ----
FilteredDF$Q9 <- factor(
  FilteredDF$Q9,
  levels = c("Yes", "No", "Not sure")
)

# ---- 2) Compute frequencies and percentages ----
plot_df_q9 <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q9)) %>%
  count(Q9) %>%
  mutate(Percent = n / sum(n))

# ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart with publication theme ----
p_q9 <- ggplot(plot_df_q9, aes(y = fct_reorder(Q9, Percent), x = Percent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline percentage labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(Percent * 100, 1), "%")),
    hjust = -0.15, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(plot_df_q9$Percent) + 0.08),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and captions
  labs(
    title = "Q9: Interest in Future Careers in Data Science",
    subtitle = "Responses show majority interest in pursuing data science roles",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents",
    y = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages based on valid (non-missing) responses."
  ) +

  # Unified publication theme
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 4) Save as PNG ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q9_interest_in_ds_career_horizontal.png",
  plot = p_q9,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q9)

Q10: How familiar are you with the following AI technologies in data science?

Combine Q-10 in one plot

revised for the chosen color theme

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
library(tidyr)
library(grid)

# ---------------------------
# Shared Levels and Palette
# ---------------------------
levels_q10 <- c("Not at All Familiar", "Not Very Familiar", "Neutral", "Somewhat Familiar", "Very Familiar")

pal_q <- c(
  "Not at All Familiar" = "#B2182B",  # deep red (least familiar)
  "Not Very Familiar"   = "#E67E22",  # orange
  "Neutral"             = "#F1C40F",  # gold
  "Somewhat Familiar"   = "#48C9B0",  # turquoise
  "Very Familiar"       = "#117A65"   # deep teal (most familiar)
)
# --- 2️⃣ Labels and Levels ---
tech_map <- c(
  Q10_1 = "ChatGPT",
  Q10_2 = "Gemini",
  Q10_3 = "Google Colab",
  Q10_4 = "AI-powered\ndata visualization tools",
  Q10_5 = "AI-driven\nanalytics platforms"
)
# ---------------------------
# Data Preparation
# ---------------------------
df_long_q10 <- FilteredDF %>%
  pivot_longer(starts_with("Q10_"), names_to = "Item", values_to = "Response") %>%
  mutate(
    Technology = recode(Item, !!!tech_map, .default = NA_character_),
    Response   = factor(Response, levels = levels_q10)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Response), !is.na(Technology))

plot_df_q10 <- df_long_q10 %>%
  count(Technology, Response, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  group_by(Technology) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Technology) %>%
  mutate(pct_familiar = sum(pct[Response %in% c("Somewhat Familiar", "Very Familiar")], na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Technology = fct_reorder(Technology, pct_familiar, .desc = TRUE))

# ---------------------------
# Plot
# ---------------------------
p_q10 <- ggplot(plot_df_q10, aes(y = Technology, x = pct, fill = Response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +
  
 # Optional: Add centered % labels inside bars (uncomment to use)
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct > 0.04, paste0(round(pct * 100), "%"), "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "black", size = 3, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_q, guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Q10: Student Familiarity with AI Technologies in Data Science",
    subtitle = "Responses grouped by self-reported familiarity level",
    x = "Percentage of Responses",
    y = "AI Technology",
    fill = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages are computed within each technology category."
  ) +
  
  # ---------------------------
  # Enhanced Publication Theme
  # ---------------------------
  theme_classic(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray25"),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10, lineheight = 1.1),
    axis.title.x    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    axis.title.y    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray90"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.text     = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.key.size = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    plot.caption    = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0, color = "gray30"),
    plot.margin     = margin(10, 15, 45, 15)
  )

# ---------------------------
# Save High-Resolution Figure
# ---------------------------

print(p_q10)

ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q10_familiarity_horizontal_fxd.png",
  plot = p_q10,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 9.5,
  height = 6.5,
  units = "in"
)

Q10 revised

p_q10 <- ggplot(plot_df_q10, aes(y = Technology, x = pct, fill = Response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct > 0.04, paste0(round(pct * 100), "%"), "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "black", size = 3, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_q, guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  labs(
    title = "Q10: Student Familiarity with AI Technologies in Data Science",
    subtitle = "Responses grouped by self-reported familiarity level",
    x = "Percentage of Responses",
    y = "AI Technology",
    fill = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages represent the proportion of responses within each technology category."
  ) +
  theme_pub()

##################

print(p_q10)

ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q10_familiarity_horizontal_fxd.png",
  plot = p_q10,
  dpi = 700,
  width = 9.5,
  height = 6.5,  # slightly taller for legend space
  units = "in"
)

Q11: Which AI tools have you used in your data science tasks? (Select all that apply) - Selected Choice

# Step 0: Define the exact expected options (based on your survey config)
Q11_options <- c(
  "ChatGPT",
  "Grammarly",
  "Claude and Copilot",
  "AI-powered visualization tools",
  "Copilot",
  "Gemini", 
  "Canva or AI assisted presentation tools",
  "Predictive analytics tools (e.g.DataRobot)",
  "None",
  "Others please specify"
)

# Step 1: Extract responses and clean
long_Q11 <- FilteredDF %>%
  select(Q11) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q11))

# Step 2: Create a binary indicator for each known option
for (opt in Q11_options) {
  long_Q11[[opt]] <- str_detect(long_Q11$Q11, fixed(opt))
}

# Step 3: Convert wide to long and count
Q11_summary <- long_Q11 %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(Q11_options), names_to =   "Response", values_to = "Selected") %>%
  filter(Selected) %>%
  count(Response, sort = TRUE)
# View result
# Q 11 Which AI tools have you used in your data science tasks? (Select all that apply)
print(Q11_summary)
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   Response                                    n
##   <chr>                                   <int>
## 1 ChatGPT                                    88
## 2 Grammarly                                  59
## 3 Canva or AI assisted presentation tools    42
## 4 AI-powered visualization tools             29
## 5 None                                       11
## With Percents
Q11_summary2 <- long_Q11 %>%
  count(Q11, sort = TRUE) %>%
  rename(Response = Q11, Frequency = n) %>%
  mutate(Relative_Freq = round(Frequency / 120*100, digits=1))

# View summary
print(Q11_summary2)
## # A tibble: 23 × 3
##    Response                                              Frequency Relative_Freq
##    <chr>                                                     <int>         <dbl>
##  1 ChatGPT                                                      23          19.2
##  2 ChatGPT ,Grammarly ,Canva or AI assisted presentatio…        18          15  
##  3 ChatGPT ,Grammarly                                           13          10.8
##  4 ChatGPT ,AI-powered visualization tools ,Grammarly ,…        11           9.2
##  5 None                                                         11           9.2
##  6 ChatGPT ,AI-powered visualization tools                       6           5  
##  7 ChatGPT ,Canva or AI assisted presentation tools              5           4.2
##  8 Grammarly                                                     4           3.3
##  9 ChatGPT ,AI-powered visualization tools ,Grammarly            3           2.5
## 10 AI-powered visualization tools ,Grammarly                     2           1.7
## # ℹ 13 more rows
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(forcats)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(stringr)

# ---- 1) Define expected options ----
Q11_options <- c(
  "ChatGPT",
  "Grammarly",
  "Claude and Copilot",
  "AI-powered visualization tools",
  "Copilot",
  "Gemini", 
  "Canva or AI-assisted \npresentation tools",
  "Predictive analytics tools",
  "None"
 # "Others please specify"
)

# ---- 2) Extract and clean responses ----
long_Q11 <- FilteredDF %>%
  select(Q11) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q11))

# ---- 3) Create binary indicators for each tool ----
for (opt in Q11_options) {
  long_Q11[[opt]] <- str_detect(long_Q11$Q11, fixed(opt))
}

# ---- 4) Convert wide → long and summarize ----
 n_valid <- FilteredDF%>% filter(!is.na(Q11)) %>% nrow()


# ---- 4) Summarize counts and percentages ----
Q11_summary <- long_Q11 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(Q11_options),
    names_to = "Tool",
    values_to = "Selected"
  ) %>%
  filter(Selected) %>%
  count(Tool, name = "Count") %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Count / n_valid,
    Label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")
  )

# ---- 5) Polished horizontal bar chart ----
p_q11 <- ggplot(Q11_summary, aes(
  y = fct_reorder(Tool, Percent),
  x = Percent
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline labels: counts + percents
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Label),
    hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis settings
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(Q11_summary$Percent) + 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and captions
  labs(
    title = "Q11: AI Tools Used in Data Science Tasks",
    subtitle = "Multiple selections allowed; percentages based on valid respondents",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents",
    y = "AI Tool",
    caption = paste0(
      "n = ", n_valid,
      " respondents (percentages may exceed 100% because multiple choices were allowed)."
    )
  ) +

  # Publication theme (consistent with your previous figures)
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 25, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 6) Optional: Save high-resolution figure ----
if (!dir.exists("Studnt_figs_10")) dir.create("Studnt_figs_10", recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q11_ai_tools.png",
  plot = p_q11,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

# ---- 7) Display summary table ----
Q11_summary
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   Tool                           Count Percent Label     
##   <chr>                          <int>   <dbl> <chr>     
## 1 AI-powered visualization tools    29  0.264  29 (26.4%)
## 2 ChatGPT                           88  0.8    88 (80%)  
## 3 Grammarly                         59  0.536  59 (53.6%)
## 4 None                              11  0.1    11 (10%)  
## 5 Predictive analytics tools         5  0.0455 5 (4.5%)
print(p_q11)

result_q7 <- plot_multi_response(
  data = FilteredDF,
  col = Q11,
  title = "Q11: AI Tools Used in Data Science Tasks",
  xlab = "Percentage of Respondents",
  ylab = "AI Tool"
)
## ✅ Plot saved as: Studnt_figs_10/Q11.png
print(result_q7)
## $plot

## 
## $summary
## # A tibble: 9 × 4
##   Response                                Count Percent Label     
##   <chr>                                   <int>   <dbl> <chr>     
## 1 ChatGPT                                    88  0.8    88 (80%)  
## 2 Grammarly                                  59  0.536  59 (53.6%)
## 3 Canva or AI assisted presentation tools    42  0.382  42 (38.2%)
## 4 AI-powered visualization tools             29  0.264  29 (26.4%)
## 5 None                                       11  0.1    11 (10%)  
## 6 DataRobot)                                  5  0.0455 5 (4.5%)  
## 7 Others                                      5  0.0455 5 (4.5%)  
## 8 Predictive analytics tools (e.g.            5  0.0455 5 (4.5%)  
## 9 please specify                              5  0.0455 5 (4.5%)  
## 
## $file
## [1] "Studnt_figs_10/Q11.png"

Q12_1: To what extent do you agree with the following statements regarding AI tools in data science?

Q 12 Redone same format as 10

# ---- 1) Map Q12_* to statement labels ----
statement_map <- c(
  Q12_1 = "AI tools enhance \naccuracy",
  Q12_2 = "AI tools automate \nprocessing",
  Q12_3 = "AI tools provide \nnew insights",
  Q12_4 = "AI tools lack \nemotional intelligence"
)

# ---- 2) Long format + recode ----
df_long_q12 <- FilteredDF %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Q12_"),
    names_to = "Item",
    values_to = "Response"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Statement = dplyr::recode(Item, !!!statement_map, .default = NA_character_),
    Response = factor(
      Response,
      levels = c("Strongly Disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral", "Agree", "Strongly Agree")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Response), !is.na(Statement))

# ---- 3) Compute proportions per statement ----
plot_df_q12 <- df_long_q12 %>%
  count(Statement, Response, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  group_by(Statement) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---- 4) Order statements by % favorable (Agree + Strongly Agree) ----
plot_df_q12 <- plot_df_q12 %>%
  group_by(Statement) %>%
  mutate(pct_fav = sum(pct[Response %in% c("Agree", "Strongly Agree")], na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Statement = fct_reorder(Statement, pct_fav, .desc = TRUE))

# ---- 5) Custom palette (consistent with your red→green family) ----
pal_q12 <- c(
  "Strongly Disagree" = "#B2182B",
  "Disagree"          = "#E67E22",
  "Neutral"           = "#F1C40F",
  "Agree"             = "#48C9B0",
  "Strongly Agree"    = "#117A65"
)

# ---- 6) Horizontal stacked proportion bar chart ----
p_q12 <- ggplot(plot_df_q12, aes(y = Statement, x = pct, fill = Response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Optional percentage labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct > 0.04, paste0(round(pct * 100), "%"), "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "black", size = 3, fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_q12, guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +

  labs(
    title = "Q12: Attitudes Toward AI Tools in Data Science",
    subtitle = "Responses grouped by agreement level",
    x = "Percentage of Responses",
    y = "Statement",
    fill = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages represent the proportion of responses within each statement."
  ) +

  # Apply your saved publication theme
  theme_pub()

# ---- 7) Save High-Resolution Figure ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q12_attitudes_horizontal_pub.pdf",
  plot = p_q12,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q12)

Q13: “How often do you use AI tools for the following data science tasks? (Never, Rarely, Sometimes, Often, Always)”

# ---- 1) Map Q13_* to task labels ----
task_map <- c(
  Q13_1 = "Exploratory data analysis",
  Q13_2 = "Predictive modeling",
  Q13_3 = "Data visualization",
  Q13_4 = "Automating data cleaning tasks",
  Q13_5 = "Presenting findings and reports"
)

# ---- 2) Define levels + consistent palette ----
levels_q13 <- c("Never", "Rarely", "Sometimes", "Often", "Always")
pal_q13 <- c(
  "Never"     = "#B2182B",  # red
  "Rarely"    = "#E67E22",  # orange
  "Sometimes" = "#F1C40F",  # gold
  "Often"     = "#48C9B0",  # turquoise
  "Always"    = "#117A65"   # teal green
)

# ---- 3) Reshape and clean ----
df_long_q13 <- FilteredDF %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Q13_"),
    names_to = "Item",
    values_to = "Response"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Task     = recode(Item, !!!task_map, .default = NA_character_),
    Response = factor(Response, levels = levels_q13)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Response), !is.na(Task))

# ---- 4) Calculate proportions ----
plot_df_q13 <- df_long_q13 %>%
  count(Task, Response, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  group_by(Task) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---- 5) Order tasks by % high use (Often + Always) ----
plot_df_q13 <- plot_df_q13 %>%
  group_by(Task) %>%
  mutate(pct_high = sum(pct[Response %in% c("Often", "Always")], na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Task = fct_reorder(Task, pct_high, .desc = TRUE))

# ---- 6) Horizontal stacked bar chart ----
p_q13 <- ggplot(plot_df_q13, aes(y = Task, x = pct, fill = Response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Optional: add centered % labels inside bars
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct > 0.04, paste0(round(pct * 100), "%"), "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "black", size = 3, fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_q13, guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +

  labs(
    title = "Q13: How often do you use AI tools for data science tasks?",
    subtitle = "Responses grouped by frequency of use",
    x = "Percentage of Responses",
    y = "Task",
    fill = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages represent the proportion of responses within each task."
  ) +

  # Apply your publication theme (legend below plot)
  theme_pub()

# ---- 7) Save high-resolution figure ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q13_utilization_horizontal_pub.pdf",
  plot = p_q13,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q13)

Q14: How confident are you in explaining AI-generated outputs for data analysis tasks to peers or supervisors?

FilteredDF$Q14 <- factor(FilteredDF$Q14,
  levels = c("Very Confident", "Confident", "Neutral", 
             "Not Very Confident", "Not at All Confident"))

# Frequency table
FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q14)) %>%
  tabyl(Q14)
##                   Q14  n    percent
##        Very Confident 11 0.10377358
##             Confident 32 0.30188679
##               Neutral 31 0.29245283
##    Not Very Confident 23 0.21698113
##  Not at All Confident  9 0.08490566

Stopped at Q14 redoo he rst using same color style and switch to horizontal charts

# ---- 1) Factor order and consistent color palette ----
conf_levels <- c(
  "Not at All Confident",
  "Not Very Confident",
  "Neutral",
  "Confident",
  "Very Confident"
)

conf_colors <- c(
  "Not at All Confident" = "#B2182B",  # deep red
  "Not Very Confident"   = "#E67E22",  # orange
  "Neutral"              = "#F1C40F",  # gold
  "Confident"            = "#48C9B0",  # turquoise
  "Very Confident"       = "#117A65"   # teal green
)

# ---- 2) Prepare data: counts + percentages ----
plot_df_q14 <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q14)) %>%
  mutate(Response = factor(Q14, levels = conf_levels)) %>%
  count(Response, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    pct   = n / sum(n),
    label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 0.1)
  )

# ---- 3) Horizontal 100% stacked bar with percentage labels ----
p_q14 <- ggplot(plot_df_q14,
                aes(y = "Confidence in \nExplaining AI Outputs", x = pct, fill = Response)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, color = "gray30") +

  # Percent labels inside segments (only for larger segments)
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct >= 0.03, label, "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    size = 3, fontface = "bold", color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis in percentage format
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Reverse legend order to show “Very Confident” first
  scale_fill_manual(values = conf_colors, guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +

  # Titles, labels, caption
  labs(
    title = "Q14: Ability to Explain AI-Generated Outputs",
    subtitle = "Student self-reported confidence level",
    x = "Percentage of Responses",
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Response",
#    caption = "Note: Percentages represent the proportion of respondents selecting each confidence level."
  ) +

  # Apply publication theme (legend under plot)
  theme_pub()

# ---- 4) Save high-resolution figure ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q14_explainability_horizontal_pub.pdf",
  plot = p_q14,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 4.5,   # slightly taller to fit legend
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q14)

Q15: To what extent do you agree with the following statements regarding the future of AI in data science? (Strongly Disagree, Disagree, Neutral, Agree, Strongly Agree)

# ---- 1) Define recode map and consistent palette ----
statement_map_q15 <- c(
  Q15_1 = "AI tools essential \nfor future workflows",
  Q15_2 = "AI improves efficiency \nin large datasets",
  Q15_3 = "AI makes DS accessible \nto non-technical professionals",
  Q15_4 = "Overreliance on AI \nmay diminish skills"
)

levels_q15 <- c("Strongly Disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral", "Agree", "Strongly Agree")
pal_q15 <- c(
  "Strongly Disagree" = "#B2182B",  # deep red
  "Disagree"          = "#E67E22",  # orange
  "Neutral"           = "#F1C40F",  # gold
  "Agree"             = "#48C9B0",  # turquoise
  "Strongly Agree"    = "#117A65"   # teal green
)

# ---- 2) Reshape and clean ----
df_long_q15 <- FilteredDF %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Q15_"),
    names_to = "Item",
    values_to = "Response"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Statement = recode(Item, !!!statement_map_q15, .default = NA_character_),
    Response  = factor(Response, levels = levels_q15)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Response), !is.na(Statement))

# ---- 3) Calculate proportions ----
plot_df_q15 <- df_long_q15 %>%
  count(Statement, Response, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  group_by(Statement) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Statement) %>%
  mutate(pct_agree = sum(pct[Response %in% c("Agree", "Strongly Agree")], na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Statement = fct_reorder(Statement, pct_agree, .desc = TRUE))

# ---- 4) Horizontal stacked bar chart ----
p_q15 <- ggplot(plot_df_q15, aes(y = Statement, x = pct, fill = Response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Optional percentage labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct > 0.04, paste0(round(pct * 100), "%"), "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "black", size = 3, fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_q15, guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +

  labs(
    title = "Q15: Attitudes about the Future of AI in Data Science",
    subtitle = "Responses grouped by agreement level",
    x = "Percentage of Responses",
    y = "Statement",
    fill = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages represent the proportion of responses within each statement."
  ) +

  # Apply your saved publication theme (with bottom legend)
  theme_pub()

# ---- 5) Save as high-resolution PNG ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q15_attitudes_horizontal_pub.png",
  plot = p_q15,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q15)

Q16: Do you think students should learn how to use AI tools for their data science careers?

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
library(janitor)

# ---- 1) Define ordered factor levels ----
FilteredDF$Q16 <- factor(FilteredDF$Q16,
  levels = c(
    "Yes, it's essential",
    "Yes, but not a priority",
    "Neutral",
    "No, it's not necessary",
    "No, it may even be harmful"
  )
)

# ---- 2) Compute frequencies and percentages ----
n_valid <- FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q16)) %>% nrow()

plot_df_q16 <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q16)) %>%
  count(Q16, name = "Count") %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Count / n_valid,
    Label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")
  )

# ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart (publication style) ----
p_q16 <- ggplot(plot_df_q16, aes(
  y = fct_reorder(Q16, Percent),
  x = Percent
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline count + percent labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Label),
    hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(plot_df_q16$Percent) + 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and captions
  labs(
    title = "Q16: Should Students Learn AI Tools for Their Data Science Careers?",
    subtitle = "Percentages based on valid (non-missing) responses",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents",
    y = "Response",
    caption = paste0("n = ", n_valid, " valid responses.")
  ) +

  # Unified publication theme
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 25, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 4) Save high-resolution plot ----
if (!dir.exists("Studnt_figs_10")) dir.create("Studnt_figs_10", recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q16_ai_learning_importance_horizontal_pub.png",
  plot = p_q16,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

# ---- 5) View table and plot ----
print(p_q16)

plot_df_q16
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   Q16                     Count Percent Label     
##   <fct>                   <int>   <dbl> <chr>     
## 1 Yes, it's essential        33  0.324  33 (32.4%)
## 2 Yes, but not a priority    59  0.578  59 (57.8%)
## 3 Neutral                    10  0.0980 10 (9.8%)

Q16 updated

Q17: To what extent do you agree with the following statement: I am willing to incorporate AI tools more into my data science projects in the future.

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
library(janitor)

# ---- 1) Define factor order ----
FilteredDF$Q17 <- factor(FilteredDF$Q17,
  levels = c("Strongly Agree", "Agree", "Neutral", "Disagree", "Strongly Disagree")
)

# ---- 2) Compute frequency and percentages ----
plot_df_q17 <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q17)) %>%
  count(Q17) %>%
  mutate(Percent = n / sum(n))

# ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart with unified publication theme ----
p_q17 <- ggplot(plot_df_q17, aes(y = fct_reorder(Q17, Percent), x = Percent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline percentage labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(Percent * 100, 1), "%")),
    hjust = -0.15, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(plot_df_q17$Percent) + 0.08),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and labels
  labs(
    title = "Q17: Willingness to Incorporate AI Tools in Future Data Science Projects",
    subtitle = "Responses ordered by level of agreement",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents",
    y = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages calculated from valid responses."
  ) +

  # Apply your shared publication theme
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 20, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 4) Save as high-resolution PNG ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q17_integration_horizontal.png",
  plot = p_q17,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q17)

Q17 updated

result_Q17 <- plot_multi_response(
  data = FilteredDF,
  col = Q17,
  title = "Q17: Willingness to Incorporate AI Tools into DS Projects",
  xlab = "Percentage of Respondents",
  ylab = "Response"
)
## ✅ Plot saved as: Studnt_figs_10/Q17.png
# Display the plot
result_Q17$plot

# View the summary table
  print(result_Q17$summary)
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   Response          Count Percent Label     
##   <chr>             <int>   <dbl> <chr>     
## 1 Agree                55 0.524   55 (52.4%)
## 2 Neutral              21 0.2     21 (20%)  
## 3 Strongly Agree       21 0.2     21 (20%)  
## 4 Disagree              7 0.0667  7 (6.7%)  
## 5 Strongly Disagree     1 0.00952 1 (1%)

Q18: To what extent are you aware of the following limitations of using AI for data science tasks? (Very Aware, Somewhat Aware, Neutral, Not Very Aware, Not at All Aware)

# # Reshape Q18 into long format
# df_Q18 <- FilteredDF %>%
#   pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("Q18_"),
#                names_to = "Limitation",
#                values_to = "Response") %>%
#   mutate(
#     Response = factor(Response,
#                       levels = c("Very Aware", "Somewhat Aware", "Neutral",
#                                  "Not Very Aware", "Not at All Aware")),
#     Limitation = fct_recode(Limitation,
#       "AI tools produce biased results" = "Q18_1",
#       "AI tools generate factually incorrect outputs" = "Q18_2",
#       "AI models lack real-world experience/context" = "Q18_3",
#       "AI results need validation by experts" = "Q18_4",
#       "AI tools pose data privacy risks" = "Q18_5"
#     )
#   )
# 
# # Frequency table
# df_Q18 %>%
#   filter(!is.na(Response)) %>%
#   count(Limitation, Response) %>%
#   group_by(Limitation) %>%
#   mutate(Percent = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1))
#

Q18 horizontal

# ---- 1) Define recode map ----
limitation_map <- c(
  Q18_1 = "Biased results",
  Q18_2 = "Factually incorrect outputs",
  Q18_3 = "Lack real-world \nexperience/context",
  Q18_4 = "Need validation \nby experts",
  Q18_5 = "Data privacy risks"
)

# ---- 2) Define factor levels + consistent palette ----
levels_q18 <- c(
  "Not at All Aware",
  "Not Very Aware",
  "Neutral",
  "Somewhat Aware",
  "Very Aware"
)

pal_q18 <- c(
  "Not at All Aware" = "#B2182B",  # deep red
  "Not Very Aware"   = "#E67E22",  # orange
  "Neutral"          = "#F1C40F",  # gold
  "Somewhat Aware"   = "#48C9B0",  # turquoise
  "Very Aware"       = "#117A65"   # teal green
)

# ---- 3) Reshape and clean ----
df_long_q18 <- FilteredDF %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Q18_"),
    names_to = "Item",
    values_to = "Response"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Limitation = recode(Item, !!!limitation_map, .default = NA_character_),
    Response   = factor(Response, levels = levels_q18)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Response), !is.na(Limitation))

# ---- 4) Calculate proportions ----
plot_df_q18 <- df_long_q18 %>%
  count(Limitation, Response, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  group_by(Limitation) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---- 5) Order by % high awareness (Somewhat + Very Aware) ----
plot_df_q18 <- plot_df_q18 %>%
  group_by(Limitation) %>%
  mutate(pct_high = sum(pct[Response %in% c("Somewhat Aware","Very Aware")], na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Limitation = fct_reorder(Limitation, pct_high, .desc = TRUE))

# ---- 6) Horizontal stacked bar chart ----
p_q18 <- ggplot(plot_df_q18, aes(y = Limitation, x = pct, fill = Response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Optional percentage labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct > 0.04, paste0(round(pct * 100), "%"), "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "black", size = 3, fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_q18, guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +

  labs(
    title = "Q18: Awareness of AI Limitations in Data Science",
    subtitle = "Responses grouped by awareness level",
    x = "Percentage of Responses",
    y = "AI Limitation",
    fill = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages represent the proportion of responses within each limitation category."
  ) +

  # Apply your saved publication theme
  theme_pub()

# ---- 7) Save as high-resolution PNG ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q18_awareness_horizontal_pub.png",
  plot = p_q18,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q18)

Q19: What are your primary concerns about using AI tools in data science?

library(stringr) 
# Create new variables (binary indicators) for each concern
FilteredDF <- FilteredDF %>%
  mutate( concern_accuracy = str_detect(Q19, regex("accuracy", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_transparency = str_detect(Q19, regex("transparency", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_privacy = str_detect(Q19, regex("privacy", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_ethics = str_detect(Q19, regex("ethic|bias", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_skill_loss = str_detect(Q19, regex("skill", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_understanding = str_detect(Q19, regex("understanding", ignore_case = TRUE)), 
concern_none = str_detect(Q19, regex("don't|none", ignore_case = TRUE)) ) 
# Count totals for each concern
q19_summary <- tibble( Concern = c("Accuracy & Reliability", "Lack of Transparency", "Privacy & Security", "Ethical Concerns (Bias)", "Skill Degradation", "Difficulty Understanding Models", "Don't Know/None"),
                       Count = c( sum(FilteredDF$concern_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_transparency, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_privacy, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_ethics, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_skill_loss, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_understanding, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_none, na.rm = TRUE) ) ) 
# Add percentages

n_valid <- FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q19)) %>% nrow()
q19_summary <- q19_summary %>% mutate(Percent = round(Count / n_valid * 100, 1)) 
# Show summary table q19_summary 
# Bar chart 
p_q19 <-ggplot(q19_summary, 
                         aes(x = fct_reorder(Concern, Count),
                             y = Percent)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  ggtitle("Q19: Primary Concerns About Using AI \n Tools in Data Science") + 
  xlab("Concern") +
  ylab("Percent of Students (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + 
  theme( axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"), 
         axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
         plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14), legend.title= element_text(face = "bold") )
print(p_q19) 

# ---- 7) Save as rectangular figure ----
ggsave("Studnt_figs_10/Q19_Primary_Concerns.pdf", plot = p_q19, width = 8.5, height = 5.5, units = "in")

Q19 redo

library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(stringr)
library(tibble)
library(stringr) 
# Create new variables (binary indicators) for each concern
FilteredDF <- FilteredDF %>%
  mutate( concern_accuracy = str_detect(Q19, regex("accuracy", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_transparency = str_detect(Q19, regex("transparency", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_privacy = str_detect(Q19, regex("privacy", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_ethics = str_detect(Q19, regex("ethic|bias", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_skill_loss = str_detect(Q19, regex("skill", ignore_case = TRUE)), concern_understanding = str_detect(Q19, regex("understanding", ignore_case = TRUE)), 
concern_none = str_detect(Q19, regex("don't|none", ignore_case = TRUE)) ) 
# Count totals for each concern
q19_summary <- tibble( Concern = c("Accuracy & Reliability", "Lack of Transparency", "Privacy & Security", "Ethical Concerns (Bias)", "Skill Degradation", "Difficulty Understanding Models", "Don't Know/None"),
                       Count = c( sum(FilteredDF$concern_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_transparency, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_privacy, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_ethics, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_skill_loss, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_understanding, na.rm = TRUE), sum(FilteredDF$concern_none, na.rm = TRUE) ) ) 
# Add percentages




n_valid <- FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q19)) %>% nrow()
q19_summary <- q19_summary %>% mutate(Percent = round(Count / n_valid * 100, 1)) 
# ---- 2) Summarize + add percentages ----
q19_summary <- tibble(
  Concern = c("Accuracy & Reliability", 
              "Lack of Transparency", 
              "Privacy & Security", 
              "Ethical Concerns (Bias)", 
              "Skill Degradation", 
              "Difficulty Understanding Models", 
              "Don't Know / None"),
  Count = c(
    sum(FilteredDF$concern_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$concern_transparency, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$concern_privacy, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$concern_ethics, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$concern_skill_loss, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$concern_understanding, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$concern_none, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
) %>%
  mutate(Percent = Count / n_valid)

# ---- 3) Polished horizontal bar chart ----
p_q19 <- ggplot(q19_summary, aes(y = fct_reorder(Concern, Percent), x = Percent)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline % labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")),
    hjust = -0.15, size = 3.6, fontface = "bold", color = "black"
  ) +

  # Axes formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(q19_summary$Percent) + 0.250),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.03))
  ) +

  # Titles and caption
  labs(
    title = "Q19: Primary Concerns About Using AI Tools in DS",
  #  subtitle = "Top issues mentioned by respondents",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents",
    y = "Concern",
     caption = paste0("n = ", n_valid,
       " respondents (multiple choices were allowed).")
  ) +

  # Unified publication theme
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 45, 45, 15)
  )

# ---- 4) Save as high-resolution PNG ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q19_primary_concerns.png",
  plot = p_q19,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5, height = 5.5, units = "in"
)

print(p_q19)

Q20: Have you ever encountered issues such as hallucinations, bias, or inaccuracy while using AI tools for data science tasks?

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
library(janitor)  # for tabyl (optional)

# ---- 1) Define factor order ----
# ---- 1) Define factor order ----
FilteredDF$Q20 <- factor(FilteredDF$Q20,
  levels = c(
    "Yes, frequently", 
    "Yes, occasionally", 
    "No, never", 
    "I never used AI tools for data science"
  )
)

# ---- 2) Compute frequency and percentages ----
n_valid <- FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q20)) %>% nrow()

Q20_summary <- FilteredDF %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q20)) %>%
  count(Q20) %>%
  rename(Response = Q20, Count = n) %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = round(Count / n_valid * 100, 1)  # percent of respondents
  )

# View summary
  
print(Q20_summary)
## # A tibble: 4 × 3
##   Response                               Count Percent
##   <fct>                                  <int>   <dbl>
## 1 Yes, frequently                           16    15.5
## 2 Yes, occasionally                         55    53.4
## 3 No, never                                 19    18.4
## 4 I never used AI tools for data science    13    12.6
# ---- 3) Horizontal bar chart with refined theme ----
p_q20 <- ggplot(Q20_summary, aes(
  y = fct_reorder(Response, Percent),
  x = Percent / 100   # convert percent back to proportion for plotting
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline labels showing count and percent
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent, 1), "%)")),
    hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(Q20_summary$Percent / 100) + 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and labels
  labs(
    title = "Q20: Encountered Issues with AI Tools in DS Tasks",
  #  subtitle = "Percentages based on valid respondents (one response per participant)",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents",
    y = "Response",
    caption = paste0("n = ", n_valid,
                     " respondents. Percentages are based on non-missing responses.")
  ) +

  # Match your publication-style theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 25, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 4) Save as high-resolution PNG ----
if (!dir.exists("Studnt_figs_10")) dir.create("Studnt_figs_10", recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q20_ai_tool_issues_horizontal_pub.png",
  plot = p_q20,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q20)

Q21: Do you think AI tools can significantly enhance the following aspects of your data science workflow? (Strongly Disagree, Disagree, Neutral, Agree, Strongly Agree)

q21 sorted

# ---- 1) Define recode map ----
aspect_map_q21 <- c(
  Q21_1 = "Automating repetitive \ntasks",
  Q21_2 = "Enhancing predictive \nmodeling accuracy",
  Q21_3 = "Visualizing \ncomplex datasets",
  Q21_4 = "Supporting faster \ndecision-making",
  Q21_5 = "Communicating to \nnon-technical stakeholders"
)

# ---- 2) Define factor levels + consistent palette ----
levels_q21 <- c("Strongly Disagree", "Disagree", "Neutral", "Agree", "Strongly Agree")
pal_q21 <- c(
  "Strongly Disagree" = "#B2182B",  # deep red
  "Disagree"          = "#E67E22",  # orange
  "Neutral"           = "#F1C40F",  # gold
  "Agree"             = "#48C9B0",  # turquoise
  "Strongly Agree"    = "#117A65"   # teal green
)

# ---- 3) Reshape and clean ----
df_long_q21 <- FilteredDF %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("Q21_"),
    names_to = "Item",
    values_to = "Response"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Aspect = recode(Item, !!!aspect_map_q21, .default = NA_character_),
    Response = factor(Response, levels = levels_q21)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Response), !is.na(Aspect))

# ---- 4) Calculate percentages ----
plot_df_q21 <- df_long_q21 %>%
  count(Aspect, Response, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(Aspect) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---- 5) Compute favorable % for sorting ----
plot_df_q21 <- plot_df_q21 %>%
  group_by(Aspect) %>%
  mutate(pct_fav = sum(pct[Response %in% c("Agree", "Strongly Agree")], na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Aspect = fct_reorder(Aspect, pct_fav, .desc = TRUE))

# ---- 6) Horizontal stacked bar chart ----
p_q21 <- ggplot(plot_df_q21, aes(y = Aspect, x = pct, fill = Response)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline percentage labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(pct > 0.04, paste0(round(pct * 100), "%"), "")),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "black", size = 3, fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  # Format axes
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Apply consistent color palette
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal_q21, guide = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +

  # Titles and labels
  labs(
    title = "Q21: Perceived Enhancement of Data Science Workflow with AI",
    subtitle = "Responses grouped by agreement level",
    x = "Percentage of Responses",
    y = "AI-Augmented Workflow Aspect",
    fill = "Response",
    caption = "Note: Percentages represent the proportion of responses within each aspect."
  ) +

  # Apply publication theme
  theme_pub()

# ---- 7) Save as high-resolution PNG ----
ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q21_workflow_enhancement_horizontal_pub.png",
  plot = p_q21,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q21)

Q22: What concerns or challenges do you face when integrating AI tools into your data science workflow?

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# ---- 1) Create binary indicators for each challenge ----
FilteredDF <- FilteredDF %>%
  mutate(
    q22_complexity    = str_detect(Q22, regex("complexity", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    q22_accuracy      = str_detect(Q22, regex("accuracy", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    q22_understanding = str_detect(Q22, regex("understanding", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    q22_privacy       = str_detect(Q22, regex("privacy", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    q22_jobimpact     = str_detect(Q22, regex("job", ignore_case = TRUE))
  )


# ---- 2) Summarize counts ----
q22_summary <- tibble(
  Concern = c(
    "Complexity in \nUsing AI Tools",
    "Accuracy & Reliability \nof Predictions",
    "Understanding AI Outputs",
    "Data Security & \nPrivacy Risks",
    "Impact on \nDS Job Roles"
  ),
  Count = c(
    sum(FilteredDF$q22_complexity, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$q22_accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$q22_understanding, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$q22_privacy, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$q22_jobimpact, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)

# ---- 3) Add percentages ----
n22 <- sum(!is.na(FilteredDF$Q22))  # total number of valid respondents

q22_summary <- q22_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Count / n22,
    Label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")
  )

# ---- 4) Horizontal bar chart with refined theme ----
p_q22 <- ggplot(q22_summary, aes(
  y = fct_reorder(Concern, Percent),
  x = Percent
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline labels: counts + percents
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Label),
    hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(q22_summary$Percent) + 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles & caption
  labs(
    title = "Q22: Challenges in Integrating AI Tools into DS Workflows",
    subtitle = "Multiple responses allowed; percentages based on valid respondents",
    x = "Percentage of Students",
    y = "Challenge",
    caption = paste0(
      "n = ", n22,
      " respondents (multiple choices were allowed)."
    )
  ) +

  # Publication theme
  theme_pub() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 25, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 5) Save as high-resolution PNG ----
if (!dir.exists("Studnt_figs_10")) dir.create("Studnt_figs_10", recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q22_challenges_workflow_pub.png",
  plot = p_q22,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

# ---- 6) View table and plot ----
print(p_q22)

q22_summary
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   Concern                                   Count Percent Label     
##   <chr>                                     <int>   <dbl> <chr>     
## 1 "Complexity in \nUsing AI Tools"              9  0.0882 9 (8.8%)  
## 2 "Accuracy & Reliability \nof Predictions"    49  0.480  49 (48%)  
## 3 "Understanding AI Outputs"                   17  0.167  17 (16.7%)
## 4 "Data Security & \nPrivacy Risks"            20  0.196  20 (19.6%)
## 5 "Impact on \nDS Job Roles"                    7  0.0686 7 (6.9%)

Q22 with counts

Q23: What concerns do you have about integrating AI tools into education in general?

# ---- 1) Create binary indicators for each concern ----
FilteredDF <- FilteredDF %>%
  mutate(
    q23_undermines_value = str_detect(Q23, regex("undermine|value", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    q23_limits_social    = str_detect(Q23, regex("interact|social", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    q23_hinders_skills   = str_detect(Q23, regex("skills|teamwork|problem", ignore_case = TRUE)),
    q23_overreliance     = str_detect(Q23, regex("over[- ]?reliant", ignore_case = TRUE))
  )

# ---- 2) Summarize counts ----
q23_summary <- tibble(
  Concern = c(
    "Undermines Value of \nUniversity Education",
    "Limits Social \nInteraction",
    "Hinders Development \nof Transferable Skills",
    "Over-Reliance on \nGenerative AI"
  ),
  Count = c(
    sum(FilteredDF$q23_undermines_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$q23_limits_social, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$q23_hinders_skills, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(FilteredDF$q23_overreliance, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)

# ---- 3) Add percentages ----
n_valid <- nrow(FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q23))) # or replace with total respondents

q23_summary <- q23_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Count / n_valid,
    Label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")
  )

# ---- 4) Horizontal bar chart with refined theme ----
p_q23 <- ggplot(q23_summary, aes(
  y = fct_reorder(Concern, Percent),
  x = Percent
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline count + percent labels
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Label),
    hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(q23_summary$Percent) + 0.1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and captions
  labs(
    title = "Q23: Concerns About Integrating AI into Education",
    subtitle = "Multiple selections allowed; percentages based on valid respondents",
    x = "Percentage of Students",
    y = "Concern",
    caption = paste0(
      "n = ", n_valid,
      " respondents (percentages may exceed 100% because multiple choices were allowed)."
    )
  ) +

  # Publication-style theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 25, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 5) Save plot ----


ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q23_ai_concerns_horizontal_pub.png",
  plot = p_q23,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

print(p_q23)

##Q23 pub SKIP Did not look good

#   title = "Q23: Concerns About Integrating AI into Education",
#   xlab = "Percentage of Respondents",
#   ylab = "Concern"

Q24: Do you believe that AI tools should complement traditional methods in data science, or replace them?

Q24 horizontal

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(forcats)
library(stringr)


# ---- 1) Wrap labels for readability ----
FilteredDF$Q24 <- str_wrap(FilteredDF$Q24, width = 35)

# ---- 2) Split and tidy multi-response data ----
long_q24 <- FilteredDF %>%
  select(Q24) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Q24)) %>%
  mutate(Q24 = str_split(Q24, ",")) %>%
  unnest(Q24) %>%
  mutate(Q24 = str_trim(Q24))

# ---- 3) Compute counts and % of valid respondents ----
n_valid <- nrow(FilteredDF %>% filter(!is.na(Q24)))   # number of respondents who answered Q24

plot_df_q24 <- long_q24 %>%
  count(Q24, name = "Count") %>%
  mutate(
    Percent = Count / n_valid,
    Label = paste0(Count, " (", round(Percent * 100, 1), "%)")
  )

# ---- 4) Horizontal bar chart (publication style) ----
p_q24 <- ggplot(plot_df_q24, aes(
  y = fct_reorder(Q24, Percent),
  x = Percent
)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#48C9B0", color = "gray30", width = 0.7) +

  # Inline labels: count + percent
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Label),
    hjust = -0.1, fontface = "bold", size = 3.6, color = "black"
  ) +

  # X-axis formatting
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = label_percent(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, max(plot_df_q24$Percent) + 0.2),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))
  ) +

  # Titles and captions
  labs(
    title = "Q24: Should AI Tools Complement or \nReplace Traditional Methods?",
    # subtitle = "Multiple selections allowed; \npercentages based on valid respondents",
    x = "Percentage of Respondents",
    y = "Response",
  #   caption = paste0(
  #     "n = ", n_valid,
  #     " respondents."
  #   )
  # ) +

   caption = paste0(
      "n = ", n_valid,
      " respondents (percentages may exceed 100% because multiple choices were allowed).")
    )+
  # Publication theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10.5, lineheight = 1.1),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 25, 25, 15)
  )

# ---- 5) Save high-resolution figure ----
if (!dir.exists("Studnt_figs_10")) dir.create("Studnt_figs_10", recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  filename = "Studnt_figs_10/Q24_complement_or_replace_horizontal_pub.png",
  plot = p_q24,
  dpi = 600,
  width = 8.5,
  height = 5.5,
  units = "in"
)

# ---- 6) Output summary table ----
plot_df_q24
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   Q24                                                        Count Percent Label
##   <chr>                                                      <int>   <dbl> <chr>
## 1 "AI tools complement traditional\nmethods"                    32  0.314  32 (…
## 2 "AI tools do not complement\ntraditional data science too…     6  0.0588 6 (5…
## 3 "AI tools mostly replace traditional\nmethods"                 6  0.0588 6 (5…
## 4 "Balance between AI tools and\ntraditional methods"           56  0.549  56 (…
## 5 "Rely entirely on AI tools"                                    2  0.0196 2 (2…
print(p_q24)

#
